name: Stashpad Classic

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Stashpad Classic 0.102.24

Performance + navigation back-ports (stable-API only, no encryption).

## Improved

- **Instant note creation — no flash.** Creating a note used to briefly show the
  filename, then re-read the body over the network before showing it (a visible
  body→title→body flash on network drives / sync). New notes now paint their real
  body immediately, and the follow-up recovery-field write no longer triggers a
  second flash.
- **Jump to any breadcrumb level.** A new button at the start of the breadcrumb row
  (always visible, even when the path is deep and the crumbs are clipped) opens a
  list of every level in the path — full titles, no truncation — so you can jump
  straight to any ancestor.

## Compatibility

`minAppVersion` stays at **1.7.0** — installs on the current stable Obsidian desktop
release and older.

> ⚠️ **Alpha software, AI-built.** Not human-audited. Keep backups.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Stashpad Classic 0.102.23

More back-ported features from mainline (all stable-API, no encryption).

## New

- **Per-folder tab icons.** Right-click a folder in the folder panel → **Set tab
  icon…** and enter a [Lucide](https://lucide.dev) icon id (e.g. `rocket`, `star`).
  The folder's tab, switcher, and panel row show it instead of the default.
- **Copy text / Cut note in the right-click menu.** Both now appear on every note's
  menu, and the focused-note header opens that same menu.
- **Deep-link polish:** a copy-link button on each note row (desktop); **Copy Stashpad
  link** with several notes selected now copies one link per note; and a new **Open
  Stashpad link** command lets you paste a deep link to jump to it (for apps that
  won't render `obsidian://` as clickable).

## Fixed

- **List timestamps honor your date format.** The two-line timestamp in the notes
  list ignored the "Date display format" setting — now it follows the dropdown
  (and timezone), matching the detail panel.

## Compatibility

`minAppVersion` stays at **1.7.0** — installs on the current stable Obsidian desktop
release and older.

> ⚠️ **Alpha software, AI-built.** Not human-audited. Keep backups.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Stashpad Classic 0.102.22

A rendering bugfix + cleanup.

## Fixed

- **No more stuck "earlier version" of a note.** The body-render cache is keyed by
  modified-time; on a network drive or after an external/synced edit, a render could
  stamp the new time onto old content and then keep showing a truncated,
  attachment-less earlier version until you reloaded. Edited notes now drop that cache
  entry so the view recomputes from fresh content.
- Removed a leftover debug log that printed on every search.

## Compatibility

`minAppVersion` stays at **1.7.0** — installs on the current stable Obsidian desktop
release and older.

> ⚠️ **Alpha software, AI-built.** Not human-audited. Keep backups.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Stashpad Classic 0.102.21

Folder-panel improvements + a review-lint fix.

## New

- **Pinned notes in the folder switcher.** New setting (off by default) — when on,
  the folder switcher / creator also lists your pinned notes so you can jump straight
  to one, not just to folders.
- **Reveal a folder's pinned notes in the panel.** Each folder row in the folder
  panel now shows a chevron + count when the folder has Home (top-level) list-pinned
  notes; click to expand an inline list and jump to any of them.

## Fixed

- Internal: a style was set directly instead of via `setCssStyles` (community-review
  lint) — no visible change.

## Compatibility

`minAppVersion` stays at **1.7.0** — installs on the current stable Obsidian desktop
release and older.

> ⚠️ **Alpha software, AI-built.** Not human-audited. Keep backups.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Stashpad Classic 0.102.20

Sync fix + panel/navigation improvements.

## Fixed

- **Settings now sync across devices.** Folder-panel pins/hides, toggles, and
  keybindings weren't syncing — Classic stored its `data.json` in a private folder
  that Obsidian Sync doesn't cover (a leftover of the removed encryption design).
  Settings now live at the standard, synced path; your existing settings migrate
  automatically on first launch (a `.bak` is kept for safety).
- **Search results return you to where you searched from.** Closing a note you
  opened from search now focuses the tab you searched from, instead of the tab to
  its right. (The other open-in-new-tab paths already did this.)

## New

- **Create a Stashpad folder from the folder panel.** A folder-plus button in the
  Folders heading opens a quick name prompt, creates the folder (seeded with a Home
  note), and opens it — no trip to Settings.

## Compatibility

`minAppVersion` stays at **1.7.0** — installs on the current stable Obsidian desktop
release and older.

> ⚠️ **Alpha software, AI-built.** Not human-audited. Keep backups.

> Note: the first cross-device launch after this update does a one-time
> last-writer-wins on settings (the migrating device's `data.json` wins); after that,
> sync is normal.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Stashpad Classic 0.102.19

Mobile fixes back-ported from mainline Stashpad — the low-risk, self-contained ones.

## Fixed (mobile)

- **First tap after typing lands on the right note.** After sending a note and
  dismissing the keyboard, the list's touch region could be left out of sync, so the
  first tap opened a note a full screen away. The list now re-syncs its tap targets
  once the keyboard settles.
- **Focused-note header no longer clips its buttons.** The action buttons (⋯ / kebab)
  could have their top edge shaved off on-device; the header now renders them fully.
- **Less flicker.** List and composer animations are disabled on mobile to cut the
  repaint flash on re-render, scroll, and selection changes.

## Not included

The larger mobile *layout* overhaul from mainline (bottom toolbars, nav controls in
the composer, 3-section rows, compact mode) is intentionally left out of this release:
it's a big structural change that needs on-device testing to ship safely.

## Compatibility

`minAppVersion` stays at **1.7.0** — installs on the current stable Obsidian desktop
release and older.

> ⚠️ **Alpha software, AI-built.** Not human-audited. Keep backups.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Stashpad Classic 0.102.18

Feature back-port: pulls several improvements from mainline Stashpad into Classic —
all using only stable-Obsidian APIs (no insider-only APIs, no encryption).

## New

- **Deep links.** Copy an `obsidian://stashpad?…` link to any note (command **Copy
  Stashpad link**, or the note's right-click menu). Click it from anywhere — another
  app, a daily note, a browser — to jump straight back to that note. Links use the
  note's stable id, so they survive renames.

## Improved

- **Smarter search results.** The note picker now ranks matches by relevance (exact
  title → title prefix → phrase → tokens → body) and then by most-recently-edited,
  instead of raw tree order. An empty query lists your most recent notes first.
- **Breadcrumbs show real titles.** A note with no heading now shows its first body
  line in the breadcrumb and tab title, instead of its filename slug.
- **Hotkeys fire more reliably.** Shift+digit and (macOS) Alt/Option+letter bindings
  now register correctly, and single-key symbol bindings like `&` (merge) work.
- **Default "Toggle task" hotkey is now G** (was H); existing bindings migrate once
  and any rebind you make afterward sticks.

## Fixed

- **Note IDs never collide.** New notes are now guaranteed a unique id at creation
  (checked against every id in the vault), eliminating the small-but-real chance of
  a duplicate id in large vaults.

## Compatibility

`minAppVersion` stays at **1.7.0** — installs on the current stable Obsidian desktop
release and older.

> ⚠️ **Alpha software, AI-built.** Not human-audited. Keep backups. Please report
> anything that misbehaves on stable Obsidian.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Stashpad Classic 0.102.17

Compatibility release: **Stashpad Classic now uses only APIs available in stable
Obsidian**, so it installs and runs on the current public desktop release and every
version back to 1.7 — no insider build required.

## What changed

- **Encryption fully removed.** All encryption features are gone — commands, the
  Encryption settings tab, folder/note context-menu options, archive-folder
  auto-encryption, the encrypted trash, and the OS-keychain (`secretStorage`)
  integration. Encryption was the feature most tied to newer/insider-only APIs.
- **No more newer-than-stable API usage.** Removed or reworked every call the
  community-plugin review flagged as newer than the plugin's supported Obsidian
  version (`secretStorage`, the async `workspace.revealLeaf` signature, and the
  1.13 declarative-settings `update()` refresh). Behavior is unchanged on the
  versions where those APIs existed; the plugin no longer *requires* them.
- **Safer HTML + cleaner source.** Cached note bodies re-hydrate through Obsidian's
  `sanitizeHTMLToDom` (no `createContextualFragment`), and a literal byte-order-mark
  in a regex is now escaped.

## Compatibility

`minAppVersion` stays at **1.7.0**, so Stashpad Classic installs on the widest range
of stable Obsidian — the current public desktop release and older — and will keep
working when 1.13 eventually reaches the stable channel.

> ⚠️ **Alpha software, AI-built.** The code was written by an AI assistant and is not
> human-audited. Keep backups. Please report anything that misbehaves.

releases.author: grub-basket
releases.description:
# Stashpad Classic 0.102.16

**The first release of Stashpad Classic** — a fork of Stashpad for **stable
(non-insider) Obsidian**.

Mainline Stashpad raised its `minAppVersion` to `1.13.0` (insider builds only) from
0.102.19 onward. Stashpad Classic is pinned at **0.102.16**, the last release that
still targets stable Obsidian (`minAppVersion 1.7.0`), so you can run it on a normal,
publicly released Obsidian.

## What's different from mainline 0.102.16

- **Encryption removed.** All encryption features are disabled in Classic — the
  lock/unlock commands, the Encryption settings tab, the folder and note
  context-menu options, archive-folder auto-encryption, and the encrypted trash.
  Encryption was the feature most tied to insider-only APIs, so Classic drops it.
- Everything else is the full Stashpad 0.102.16 feature set: chat-style nested notes,
  outliner navigation, fast search, tasks, per-folder templates, and one-click
  **Open Knowledge Format (OKF)** export.

## Install

Via [BRAT](https://github.com/TfTHacker/obsidian42-brat): add the beta plugin
`grub-basket/SP-Classic`. Or download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css`
from this release into `.obsidian/plugins/stashpad-classic/` in your vault.

> ⚠️ **Alpha software, AI-built.** Stashpad does a lot and is young; the code was
> written by an AI assistant and is not human-audited. Keep backups. This is the
> first Classic build — please report anything that misbehaves on stable Obsidian.
